the number of times 2 appears in 100! is calculated by summing the floor division of 100 by 2, then by 4, then by 8, and so on until the division results in zero. this gives us the total exponent of 2 in 100!. similarly, the number of times 5 appears in 100! is calculated by summing the floor division of 100 by 5, then by 25, then by 125, and so on. this gives us the total exponent of 5 in 100!.  

after calculating these exponents, we subtract the exponents from 10^{50} to find the remaining exponents of 2 and 5 in the denominator. the remaining exponents of 2 and 5 in the denominator will be the factors that remain after simplifying the fraction.  

the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be the product of the remaining exponents of 2 and 5. this means that the denominator is $2^a \times 5^b$, where a and b are the exponents of 2 and 5 in 100! after subtracting the exponents from 10^{50}.
